title: CNN-RNN Encoder-Decoder Models
concept: Classical captioning models use a CNN encoder to extract spatial features and an RNN/LSTM decoder to generate the corresponding text sequence word-by-word.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main output format of an image captioning system?

options.text: A natural language sentence describing the scene.

exercises.explain: Correct! Captioning generates complete, grammatical strings rather than coordinates or tags.

cards.title: Encoding and decoding
cards.body: How do we connect pixels to words? The most standard way is a two-step framework. First, a visual network translates the image into an abstract summary. Then, a language network reads that summary and generates a sequence of words, one by one, until the sentence is complete.

cards.title: The Encoder-Decoder Pipeline
cards.body: In a classical setup, a CNN Encoder (like ResNet or Inception) extracts high-level spatial features from the image and projects them into a vector. An RNN/LSTM Decoder takes this vector as its initial state and predicts the sequence of words sequentially (word-by-word) until it outputs a stop token.

cards.title: The summary and the storyteller
cards.body: The CNN encoder is like an analyst who reads a long report and writes a one-paragraph bulleted summary (feature vector). The RNN decoder is like a storyteller who takes that summary and writes an engaging, full-length narrative, choosing one word at a time based on the previous words.

cards.title: 'Show and Tell' model
cards.body: Google's landmark 2015 'Show and Tell' model used a pre-trained CNN (like Inception) stripped of its classification head. The final convolutional features were mapped to an LSTM input, which then predicted words sequentially from a vocabulary.

cards.title: The CNN does not classify
cards.body: You might think the CNN encoder output is a class label (like 'dog'). In fact, the final classification layer is removed. The CNN outputs raw **activation maps** (dense feature representations), preserving complex details like colors, shapes, and positions for the decoder to use.

cards.title: CNN-RNN pipeline data flow
cards.body: The process of generating a sentence word-by-word:

left.text:
Extracts visual feature map
Collapses pixels to feature vector
Passes vector to language state

right.text:
Takes visual state
Predicts Word 1 -> feeds back to state
Predicts Word 2 -> repeats till [END]

exercises.prompt: What is the role of the CNN in a classical CNN-RNN captioning model?

options.text: To act as a visual feature encoder, extracting spatial descriptors from the image
To predict the grammatical structure of the sentence
To convert text strings into speech audio

exercises.explain: The CNN acts as the encoder, capturing visual characteristics and passing them to the text decoder.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The RNN decoder predicts all words in the caption simultaneously in a single forward pass.
exercises.explain: False. RNN decoders are autoregressive, generating captions sequentially, one word at a time, feeding prior outputs back into the loop.

exercises.sentence: In classical captioning, the CNN acts as the visual encoder, while the LSTM acts as the text ___.
exercises.explain: The LSTM decodes visual features into sequential words.

exercises.prompt: Which visual representation shows a CNN-RNN encoder-decoder system in operation?

options.text: An image mapped to a 2048D feature vector, which initializes an LSTM generating words sequentially
A list of bounding box coordinates fed to a sorting algorithm
A set of facial landmarks rotated horizontally

exercises.explain: Extracting features with a CNN to initialize an LSTM sequence generator is the classic CNN-RNN setup.

exercises.prompt: You are training an image captioning model. During testing, the LSTM decoder gets stuck in a loop and outputs the word 'dog' endlessly: 'A dog on a dog dog dog...'. What should you inspect?

options.text: The decoder's text generation settings, and implement techniques like beam search or repetition penalty.
The resolution of the input image, increasing it to 4K.
The face alignment landmarks of the dog.
The learning rate of the CNN feature extractor.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Repetitive generation is a common decoder bug. Implementing beam search or adding repetition penalties forces the decoder to consider alternative word choices. Outstanding! Next: how attention maps help the model focus.
